calendar.matters:
Motion To Set Aside Default Judmgnet And To Restore Case To Civil Active List
Off Calendar Dec-21-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
RULING:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT JEFFREY MATZ' Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment And To Restore Case To Civil Active List: DENIED; this is a request for discretionary relief. The 6 month limit after entry of default is jurisdictional, default was entered on 04/23/04 and this motion was not filed until 11/22/04. This ruling is without prejudice to Defendant's right to seek equitable relief on the grounds of extrinsic fraud/mistake if he can do so in good faith. Prevailing party to submit form of order. (301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-03-2004 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up In Scd.
Off Calendar Oct-28-2004 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Carlito Mandia.
Debra Lew, Esq. for Plaintiff: State Bar of California, a Public Corp.
Plaintiff's witness: Tracey McCormick was sworn and examined.
After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant: Jeffrey Allan Matz as follows:
Principal $528,491.91, no fees or costs.
Judgment signed in open court.
